text: can you add annotation on what each data set (gray and yellow are used to define crime, in some other cities this value
producerNote: Done at the layer the watcher owns: verified the engine-wide SeamCallout ('WHY THE JUMP?', FullTrend.tsx L189-225) names each era — gray = FBI classic index crimes, city color = broader incident-based system — and states the jump is the classification ruler changing, not the city. DC re-render is tracked by an existing confidence.json blocker (feedback 2026-07-18); the render itself belongs to the driver/producer, not the watcher.

text: the value jumps in some cities when data set source changes, and it would be to have a few word on how they can be different and does crime clasification has been updated?
producerNote: Answered at DC's config layer: added a verified DC-specific seamExplain to the 'WHY THE JUMP?' card — through 2007 = DC's yearly UCR figures reported to the FBI (classic index crimes); from 2008 = MPD's own incident feed (different source/taxonomy, logged case by case, still major Part-I offenses), so the jump is the measuring system changing, not a re-classification of crime. This corrects the generic card's 'broader' overstatement for DC (MPD publishes only major offenses). Config-only change; re-render is tracked by the confidence.json blocker — GPU belongs to the producer.
